headd: Objective Test Questions: Answer all the questions

S1.headd: In each of the following sentences, there is one word or group of words underlined and one gap. From the list of words or group of words lettered A to D, choose the one that is most nearly opposite in meaning to the underlined word or group of words and that will, at the same time, correctly fill the gap in the sentence.

S1.qs: 1.	The man refused to accept the dffer of a job in the **rural** area; he preferred one in the .................... area.
2.	He is quite an **outspoken** young man but his father is ......................
3.	One of the twins was as **daring** as the other was .................
4.	A gully, which is a **natural** phenomenon should not be mistaken for a tunnel, which is ..............
5.	The crops the farmer **planted** will be ................. in three months’ time
6.	He **intentionally** hid the document but his secretary ................ burnt them
7.	It is curious how Bayo can be so **carefree** in his ways while his younger brother is so .................
8.	Personally, I **give in** rather easily when it comes to arguments, but Emeka will always ..................... to his opinion
9.	He didn’t lose the fight because of his **bravery** but for his ...................
10.	If you do not drive with **care** you may be charged for ..................

S2.headd: From the words lettered A to D, choose the word that best completes each of the following sentences.

S2.qs: 11.	When you ................ an opponent’s point in a debate, you must do so politely.
12.	Mr Collins does not allow his name to be published in connection with his philanthropy because he .............. publicity
13.	I should be as ............ as Kofi if I were so rejected by a close friend
14.	The press will no longer be free when the new government starts to ................. the newspapers
15.	Everyone was disappointed to see the pastor doing those things that were .................. with the morals he preached
16.	That boy is mentally unbalanced, so you should be ................. of his behaviour
17.	The president complained that too many doctors have .................. for better remuneration
18.	The doctor listened to my heart-beat with a ................
19.	She was ................ and it was not clear to me what she meant.
20.	Clara, your writing is difficult to read; you must try to make it ..................

S3.headd: After each of the following sentences, a list of possible interpretation is given. Choose the interpretation that you consider most appropriate for each sentence.

S3.qs: 21.	Despite their newness on the job, the workers were remunerated rather handsomely. This means that

opts.q1: B. those paid were handsome in appearance
opts.q3: D. the workers were paid by handsome accountants

S3.qs: 22.	The plan to upgrade the dispensary to a general hospital did not materialize. This means that

opts.q0: A. the arrangements did not work out as wished
opts.q1: B. the plan was badly executed
opts.q2: C. it was difficult to obtain the materials
opts.q3: D. the plan did not meet the required specifications

S3.qs: 23.	The robbery suspect said he made his earlier confessional statement under duress. This means that he made it

opts.q0: A. because he was forced to
opts.q1: B. in the presence of a judge
opts.q2: C. because he took an oath
opts.q3: D. with the help of his lawyer

S3.qs: 24.	Despite their unrefined behaviour, the visitors were given preferential treatment. This means that the visitors were

opts.q0: A. ill-treated because of their coarseness
opts.q3: D. attended to first by the doctor

S3.qs: 25.	Onome’s father blew his top at the news of his performance in the examination. This means that he
26.	There is no question of either of you getting the job,' the manager said, This means that

opts.q0: A. the manager would not ask any question about the job
opts.q1: B. both of them would get the job
opts.q2: C. only one of them would get the job
opts.q3: D. neither of them would get the job

S3.qs: 27.	Jumoke knew that her husband had had a drop too many when he asked her to pack out. This means that her husband was
28.	It was quite impossible for him not to have noticed that Lola had arrived. This means that he

opts.q0: A. should have known that Lola had arrived
opts.q1: B. found it impossible to say that Lola had arrived
opts.q2: C. said that Lola had not arrived
opts.q3: D. pretended not to know that Lola had arrived

S3.qs: 29.	My father advised us to go with the crowd. This means that he advised us not to

opts.q3: D. be unduly influenced by others

S3.qs: 30.	We would grant your request if we had money. This means that

opts.q0: A. we have the money but will not grant your request
opts.q1: B. we have the money and will grant your request
opts.q2: C. we will have the money and will grant your request
opts.q3: D. we do not have the money and cannot grant your request

S4.headd: From the words or group of words lettered A to D below each of the following sentences, choose the word or group of words that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word or group of words as it is used in the sentence.

S4.qs: 31.	The government has approved a new salary structure as an **incentive** for workers
33.	They have tried to **circumvent** the restriction on the importation of scarce commodities.
34.	The company had satisfied itself that the project was **feasible** before embarking on it
35.	Though there is no riot on the campus, the students are **restive**
36.	The prosecutor was accused of **obstructing** justice.
37.	The man preaches **humility** without matching it with action
38.	Mr. Haruna plays the piano with great **dexterity**
39.	The college officer insisted that I show him my **credentials** before I could be registered
40.	Amina said that she married a **doting** man

S5.headd: From the words or group of words lettered A to D, choose the word or group of words that best completes each of the following sentences.

S5.qs: 41.	A loyal citizen ought not to abstain ................ voting
42.	She set ....................... preparing lunch when I came
43.	The ................. engineer designed a new machine
44.	.................... were asked to represent the school at the quiz competition
45.	I am not going to the stadium with you as I .................. that team play before
46.	I won’t be able to come to the party as the day’s work has tired me ....................
47.	It's been so rough so far, ......................?
48.	The suspect ...................... when I entered the office.
49.	Ada had only ................ time to spend with he family before the plane took off.
50.	The calculations looked ................ simple that he thought he would not get any of them wrong.
51.	The story she was reading made her ...............
52.	.................. people turned out for the rally because of the poor publicity.
53.	The two brothers fell out and refused to live .................... the same roof
54.	The teacher asked, “..................... will have the courage to try and lift that heavy log?”
55.	My friend and I quarrelled last week and haven’t ...................... yet
56.	After two years of courtship, Jide is ...................... Nkechi next Sunday
57.	The visitor was very uncomfortable because of his ............. nose
58.	My friend’s car has ............... to a halt
59.	It’s time we ................ for the party
60.	Bassey was ................. by the girl’s story
61.	.................., you should put in some effort to show that you are interested.
62.	.................. ride a bicycle but he also drives a car
63.	Our plans for the trip fell ................. because we had no money.
64.	My brother ........... studying in England for two years now.
65.	I am not going to the cinema tonight, neither ................ you.
66.	No sooner had we started the journey ................. we had a flat tyre
67.	In this letter there .................. good news about your elder brother
68.	There were men on the job day and night all the time that this hotel .................
69.	I shall have to start work .................. I can after leaving school
70.	Mary and Jane are always fighting ....................
71.	Don’t let him bully you; you must stand up ...................
72.	The plane ................. at 2 o’clock yesterday
73.	It was a long time before his surprise wore ................
74.	The bananas weren’t ................. ripe enough to use today.
75.	I’m sorry I can’t find .................. of the two books you lent me.
76.	When she visited us we asked her if she .................... pounded yam for lunch

S6.headd: In the following passages, the numbered gaps indicate missing words. Against each number in the list below each passage, four options are offered in columns lettered A to D. Choose the word that is the most suitable to fill the numbered gap in the passages.
PA: Oil is perhaps the most sought-after of all --77--. However, a major difficulty in its --78-- is that oil --79-- are very often below the sea --80--.
As a result the search for oil is very expensive, and unless oil is found, there in --81-- quantities the companies or governments involved are liable to lose heavily.

Therefore, before --82-- starts, extensive surveys are carried out to see how much oil, or --83-- gas the rock formation is likely to yield. If the results are favourable an oil rig is towed into position.

The rig consists of a man --84-- on which the men work and a derrick whichs support the drill. This drill goes down thousands of feet until oil or a --85-- of gas is reached. The next stage is to transport the gas or oil to the shore.
In the case of gas this is normally done by laying a --86-- below the sea, but oil on reaching the top of the well is more often transported in its --87-- state by --88--.
On shore it is --89-- into different fuels or it is treated chemically to turn it into a variety of --90-- materials such as polythene.

S6.headd: In the following passages, the numbered gaps indicate missing words. Against each number in the list below each passage, four options are offered in columns lettered A to D. Choose the word that is the most suitable to fill the numbered gap in the passages.
PB: Publishing is a fast growing business and there are therefore many publishing houses all over the country. When --91-- is submitted by an author, the publisher sends it to --92-- to know if it is actually publishable. This step is important because the publisher wants to make sure that the book catches the --93-- when it i actually published.

In a good publishing house, there is --94-- section which is concerned with --95-- the manuscript and correcting both the spelling and typing errors. After this, the manuscript is --96-- in readiness for --97-- printing. The printed --98-- are then stored in the warehouse and a few of them may be sent to the author as --99-- copies.

Since it is not just the aim of the publisher to offset the cost of production but also to make some profit, there is a strong marketing division which promotes sales. A certain percentage of the cover price of the book is paid to the author as --100--.
